code.text: Specimen information

text.div:
Specimen information

Test08-JUL-2016Specimen
Appearance of UrineCloudy urineUrine
Odor of UrineUrine smell ammoniacalUrine
Clarity of UrineTranslucentUrine
Color of UrineBrown colorUrine

Annotation

Conclusion and Recommendations based on this report and previous findings known to us
The patient shows elevated serum creatinine, indicating impaired renal function. Urinalysis reveals significant proteinuria, glucosuria, mild ketonuria, low specific gravity, and abnormal urine appearance (cloudy, brown, ammoniacal), but no hematuria, nitrites, or leukocytes. These findings suggest possible chronic kidney disease with tubular dysfunction (possibly nephrotic syndrome or diabetic nephropathy). Recommend further nephrological evaluation and assessment for underlying causes.
